preamble: The shift from the voiced uvular stop [ɢ] to the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] is a classic example of spirantization (lenition). Due to the articulatory difficulty of maintaining voicing so far back in the vocal tract, the stop closure is frequently relaxed into a fricative or approximant.

examples.note: Intervocalic spirantization of the uvular stop into a voiced fricative.
Systematic allophonic shift where the voiced uvular stop spirantizes in most positions except after nasals.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
